entries.description: Started Session 1 via `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/023-trust-completed-sessions-array --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort low` per the state-first protocol Set 022 shipped. State file flipped currentSession null -> 1, status not-started -> in-progress, lifecycleState null -> work_in_progress; work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl.

ai_router/close_session.py: replaced the events-ledger overwrite in _run_repair Case 1's completedSessions[] backfill with a monotone-up union of (a) the snapshot's existing array (sanitized — drops non-int, booleans, negatives) and (b) the now-repaired events ledger's closeout_succeeded session numbers. Repair never drops a session number the operator hand-authored. Removed the stale 'unified-master-details-composite incident' comment block whose rationale was a bug (the cited shape would have produced the *correct* answer under union, not the wrong answer the comment claimed). The new comment block explains the union framing and points at Set 023's motivation (Set 004 migration regression on 2026-05-15).

Distinguished four apply outcomes in the repair's `messages` line so the operator can tell at a glance what happened to the snapshot's completedSessions[]: 'backfilled' (no array before), 'merged' (clean array unioned with events), 'normalized' (malformed/unsorted array cleaned + unioned), 'preserved' (no rewrite — array already correct). Round-1 verifier review added the 'normalized' branch (was originally 3 outcomes; verifier caught that malformed inputs needed their own message and rewrite path).

Added 3 regression tests to ai_router/tests/test_close_session_session4.py: test_repair_preserves_snapshot_completed_sessions_superset (snapshot [1,2,3,4] + ledger {3} -> preserved branch, message reports 'preserved completedSessions=[1, 2, 3, 4]'); test_repair_merges_snapshot_completed_sessions_with_events (snapshot [2] + ledger {1} -> [1, 2] via union, message reports 'merged completedSessions=[1, 2] (union of snapshot [2] and events [1, 2])', plus idempotent second-apply assertion); test_repair_normalizes_malformed_snapshot_completed_sessions (round-1 verifier-finding fix: snapshot [1, -1, 2] + ledger {1} -> [1, 2] with 'normalized' message). 702 tests pass (701 after first two adds, 702 after round-1 fix). Existing test_repair_detects_mixed_mode_drift continues passing — its [1, 2] assertion is preserved under the new union code via the 'backfilled' branch.

Updated docs/session-sets/023-trust-completed-sessions-array/spec.md at the operator's request: added a new Session 2 'Cross-provider design-alignment audit' between the current Session 1 (writer fix) and Session 2 -> 3 (reader fix). The new audit session routes a single design-review prompt to both GPT 5.4 and Gemini Pro with five specific questions (hand-migration safety, union monotonicity, reader ordering, sharp-edge coverage, open). Same design-round pattern that informed Set 022's spec. totalSessions bumped 2 -> 3 in the spec config block and session-state.json. The reader-fix session's progress keys renumbered session-002/* -> session-003/*.
Bumped ai_router to 0.2.4 in pyproject.toml and ai_router/__init__.py.

Updated ai_router/docs/close-out.md Section 5 drift-case-1 to enumerate the four apply outcomes (backfilled / merged / normalized / preserved) and note that the union math operates on a sanitized view of the snapshot. Round-1 verifier finding about 'Two apply outcomes' / three-listed discrepancy is resolved here.

Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4) across two rounds. Round 1 ($0.140): ISSUES_FOUND — 1 Major (preserved branch operated on sanitized comparison and could leave malformed snapshot in place; e.g., snapshot [1, -1] with ledger {1} would take 'preserved' and not rewrite); 1 Minor (close-out.md said 'Two apply outcomes' while listing three). Both fixed: split existing_raw_list from existing_clean to drive the rewrite-needed check; added 'normalized' message branch + regression test; updated close-out.md to enumerate four outcomes. Round 2 ($0.118): VERIFIED, issues: []. Total Session 1 verification spend: $0.258.

Started Session 2 via `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/023-trust-completed-sessions-array --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort low`. State flipped currentSession 1->2, work_started event appended.

Authored docs/session-sets/023-trust-completed-sessions-array/session-reviews/session-002-audit/prompt.md: structured design-review prompt covering Set 022 invariant (three-line state interpretation rule + completedSessions[] authority), the two sharp edges Set 022's migration of Sets 004+006 surfaced, the Session 1 writer fix (with diff excerpt), and the planned Session 3 reader fix (with pseudo-code). Five questions in JSON-out format: (a) hand-migration generalizes, (b) writer union monotone-up, (c) reader ordering correctness, (d) third-sharp-edge coverage, (e) open-ended. Folder named session-002-audit (operator's option in spec step 2: 'rename folder to `session-002-audit` if cleaner').

Routed the design-review prompt to gpt-5-4 via ai_router.query(model='gpt-5-4', task_type='analysis'). Saved full RouteResult dump to session-002-audit/gpt-5-4.json (memory rule: dump before attribute access). Verdicts: (a) concur-with-caveat/major (hand-migration is operator-attestation, not auto-reconstruction); (b) raise-concern/major (union admits out-of-range positives); (c) concur-with-caveat/minor (array-first correct, add drift warn); (d) raise-concern/major (third sharp edge possible — other ledger-only readers); (e) concur-with-caveat/minor (sharpen 'authoritative' doc language).

Routed the same prompt to gemini-pro via ai_router.query(model='gemini-pro', task_type='analysis'). Saved to session-002-audit/gemini-pro.json. Verdicts: (a) concur/none; (b) concur-with-caveat/minor (same out-of-range concern as GPT, milder severity); (c) concur/none (OR-logic correct; AND would re-create migration deadlock); (d) raise-concern/critical (same third-sharp-edge concern as GPT, escalated severity — systemic change in data authority, needs codebase-wide audit); (e) concur-with-caveat/minor (capture invariant in central docs, add non-array fixture).

Authored docs/session-sets/023-trust-completed-sessions-array/session-reviews/session-002-audit/audit-summary.md: per-question verdict table, agreement/disagreement narrative, tie-breaker resolutions (more conservative wins where severity differs), concrete spec refinements queued for Session 4. Flagged to operator on question (d): both providers independently raised the same third-sharp-edge concern (Gemini critical, GPT major) — does NOT trip the strict spec gate ('Critical that invalidates the writer fix') but does warrant operator scope decision between three options (A) ship + audit during Session 3/4, (B) pause and expand Set 023 to include audit, (C) ship + defer audit to separate set.

Operator chose option (B): pause Session 4 and expand Set 023's scope to include a system-wide audit. Updated spec.md and session-state.json: totalSessions 3->4. Inserted new 'Session 3 of 4: System-wide audit of events-ledger consumers' between Session 2 and the original reader-fix session; the original Session 3 became Session 4. New Session 3 steps: mechanical grep sweep across Python + TypeScript for events-ledger consumers, classify each as already-correct / correct-to-ignore / sharp-edge, document findings in session-003-audit-findings.md, fix any sharp edges (Python -> ai_router 0.2.5 ship; TypeScript -> bundle into Session 4's v0.13.13). Applied non-blocking audit refinements to Session 4 plan: observability warn when array overrides missing ledger closeout (GPT caveat on (c)); fixture F5 (non-array completedSessions, Gemini on (e)); fixture F6 (stray out-of-range entry, both on (b)+(e)); fixture F7 (non-final-session disagreement, GPT on (e)); sharpened 'authoritative' doc language for whether-vs-when distinction (both on (e)); operator-attestation note to close-out.md (GPT on (a)). Added three new Risks bullets: writer-union out-of-range gap with follow-on 0.2.5 plan, no-release-coupling renumber, system-wide-audit-deferred-to-this-set.

Cross-provider verification of audit-summary.md via gpt-5-4-mini (third model — not a subject; same OpenAI family as gpt-5-4 but different model tier; documented caveat that truly third-provider would require an Anthropic model not in the registry). Round 1 verdict ISSUES_FOUND: 1 Major (audit-summary's (b) spec-refinement contained an internally contradictory claim — 'completedSessions: [1,2,99], currentSession 4, returns false (session 4 is in the array)', which is false since [1,2,99].includes(4) is false). The spec's fixture F6 already self-corrected inline; the summary's paraphrase had not caught up. Fixed in audit-summary.md by rewriting the (b) refinement to correctly state the reader falls through to the events-ledger check and returns true (since 4 is not in the array). No round-2 re-route: the defect is a textual self-contradiction verifiable by human inspection; verifier's recommended_fix exactly matches what was applied; a re-route would duplicate $0.025 of cost on a finding any reader can confirm. Verification spend: $0.0251.
